full_text: Next element is called div element. The official name of the div element is called content division element. The basic function of this element is to divide the content. There is no functionality in this element like the paragraph element is created. Image element is created. The work of our anchor tag is to display a hyperlink. But our div element is not its own functionality. It is a container element for the rest of the elements. DIM is a container that is used to make a group of the rest of the HTML elements. So that if we want to group different elements, we can write them in a div element and group them. And we have a block level element. And I want the background color of the line that is coming on the google and youtube. For such cases, when I have to do such things, I am going to create a div element outside. I have created a div element. This is the opening tag. This is my closing tag. And I am going to transfer my anchor tags in this div element. So in a way, I have created an anchor tag in the div element. I have created an anchor element. And now I am going to save it and refresh it. So what happened in this way? Because div is a block level element. So all the items in one div will occupy this entire line. That is, google and youtube tags will occupy the entire line. And the things after the div, like the element of facebook, will come on the next line. To verify this, we can inspect that here this is my entire div. I have this anchor tag inside the div. And in the next line, I have only an anchor tag. So what did this div do? It occupied the entire line, which has the tags of google and youtube. Now I want to change the background color of the whole div. I am going to click on this element. And here I will write background color equals red. But because I want to show you an example of the div, so if I have a task in which I have to group some elements, take them on a separate line and change the background color for them, then I can use div element to group them there. And with the help of div element, they will also be grouped. And along with that, they will also go in a different line. But the main work of div or the special properties, we will not be able to understand them much now. When we are doing CSS, then we will use div more and understand its properties more. But the basic div is that div is a block-level element. So we will write all the tags within the div. They will occupy all of our lines, block, and block. So when we are designing and creating normal websites and web pages, then we will use div a lot.
